fragments.content: Substance Use Disorder (SUD) is a problematic pattern of substance use leading to clinically significant impairment/distress, manifested by ≥2 criteria within 12 months: taking larger amounts/longer than intended, persistent desire/unsuccessful efforts to cut down, time spent obtaining/using/recovering, craving, failure to fulfill major role obligations, continued use despite social/interpersonal problems, giving up activities, use in hazardous situations, continued use despite physical/psychological problems, tolerance, withdrawal. Severity: mild (2-3), moderate (4-5), severe (≥6). SUDs involve neurobiological changes in reward (dopamine mesolimbic pathway), motivation, memory circuits. Genetic factors account for ~40-60% vulnerability.

Treatment approaches vary by substance. Opioid Use Disorder: Medication for OUD (MOUD) first-line—methadone, buprenorphine, or extended-release naltrexone reduce mortality and improve outcomes; combine with counseling/behavioral therapies. Alcohol Use Disorder: naltrexone, acamprosate, disulfiram; motivational interviewing, CBT, contingency management. Tobacco: NRT, varenicline, bupropion plus behavioral support. Stimulant use: contingency management has strongest evidence; no FDA-approved medications yet. Benzodiazepines: gradual tapering. Treatment settings range from outpatient to residential based on severity. Recovery support services, peer support, and addressing co-occurring mental health conditions essential for long-term success.

DSM-5-TR SUD criteria: Problematic substance use leading to impairment/distress, ≥2 of following within 12 months: (1) larger amounts/longer than intended, (2) persistent desire/unsuccessful efforts to control use, (3) great deal of time spent obtaining/using/recovering, (4) craving, (5) failure to fulfill major role obligations, (6) continued use despite social/interpersonal problems, (7) important activities given up/reduced, (8) recurrent use in hazardous situations, (9) continued use despite physical/psychological problems, (10) tolerance, (11) withdrawal. Specifiers: in early/sustained remission, on maintenance therapy, in controlled environment. Substance-specific diagnoses (alcohol, cannabis, opioids, stimulants, sedatives, tobacco, etc.).
